metadata.name: Persist — Kitchen Finks returns once (no -1/-1 counters), stays dead on second death (has -1/-1 counter)
metadata.description: P1 controls Kitchen Finks (3/2, Persist, ETB: gain 2 life). P2 controls Bog Raiders (2/2, Swampwalk) and Elvish Mystic (1/1). P1 controls no Swamps, so Swampwalk is irrelevant and Kitchen Finks can legally block Bog Raiders. In the first combat, P2 attacks with Bog Raiders; P1 blocks with Kitchen Finks. Both take lethal damage and die simultaneously (SBA CR 704.5g). Persist triggers on Kitchen Finks (CR 702.79a): Kitchen Finks had no -1/-1 counters, so it returns to the battlefield under P1's control with a -1/-1 counter (becoming 2/1). The ETB triggered ability fires: P1 gains 2 life (40 → 42). In the second combat (new turn), P2 attacks with Elvish Mystic; P1 blocks with the persisted Kitchen Finks (now 2/1 with -1/-1 counter). Kitchen Finks takes 1 damage (toughness now 1 — lethal). Kitchen Finks dies again. This time, persist's intervening-if clause checks whether Kitchen Finks had no -1/-1 counters — it does have one — so persist does NOT trigger (CR 702.79a + CR 603.4 intervening-if). Kitchen Finks remains in the graveyard permanently. This script validates: persist trigger fires on first death, ETB chaining after persist, and persist suppression on second death due to -1/-1 counter.
metadata.generation_notes: Kitchen Finks oracle text (Scryfall verified via MCP lookup_card): 'When this creature enters, you gain 2 life. Persist (When this creature dies, if it had no -1/-1 counters on it, return it to the battlefield under its owner's control with a -1/-1 counter on it.)' Type: Creature — Ouphe. P/T: 3/2. Mana cost: {1}{G/W}{G/W}. IMPORTANT: Kitchen Finks is NOT in the engine Available Cards list. This script requires a CardDefinition for Kitchen Finks before harness validation can pass. Bog Raiders is in the Available Cards list (2/2, Swampwalk). Elvish Mystic is in the Available Cards list (1/1). CR 702.79a: Persist is a triggered ability — 'When this permanent is put into a graveyard from the battlefield, if it had no -1/-1 counters on it, return it to the battlefield under its owner's control with a -1/-1 counter on it.' The 'if it had no -1/-1 counters on it' is an intervening-if clause (CR 603.4) — checked both when the trigger would trigger AND when the trigger resolves. First combat: Bog Raiders (2/2) deals 2 damage to Kitchen Finks (toughness 2) — exactly lethal. Kitchen Finks (3/2, power 3) deals 3 damage to Bog Raiders (toughness 2) — lethal. SBA 704.5g destroys both simultaneously. Persist trigger fires on Kitchen Finks (no -1/-1 counters). ETB trigger fires when Kitchen Finks returns: P1 gains 2 life. After persist, Kitchen Finks is a 2/1 (3-1/2-1 = 2/1) with one -1/-1 counter. CR 400.7: the returned Kitchen Finks is a new object with no connection to the previous one. Second combat (turn 5, new turn to avoid summoning sickness on returned Kitchen Finks): Elvish Mystic (1/1) attacks. Kitchen Finks (2/1) blocks. Elvish Mystic deals 1 damage to Kitchen Finks (toughness 1) — lethal. Kitchen Finks deals 2 damage to Elvish Mystic (toughness 1) — lethal. Both die. Persist trigger on Kitchen Finks: intervening-if checks — Kitchen Finks HAD a -1/-1 counter → condition fails → persist does NOT fire (CR 702.79a). Kitchen Finks stays in graveyard. Ruling (2013-06-07): 'The persist ability triggers when the permanent is put into a graveyard. Its last known information (that is, how the creature last existed on the battlefield) is used to determine whether it had a -1/-1 counter on it.' Swampwalk on Bog Raiders: P1 controls no Swamps → Swampwalk is irrelevant → Kitchen Finks can legally block Bog Raiders (CR 702.14a). Turn structure: combat 1 at turn 4 (P2 active, Kitchen Finks pre-placed so no summoning sickness concern — initial state builder sets summoning sickness false). For second combat, advance to turn 5 (P2 active again) to mirror the attack pattern. NOTE: Both combats are P2 attacking P1. P2 is the active player in all combat steps, so all priority_round entries use ['p2', 'p1'] order (active player passes first, CR 116.3). Initial phase: declare_attackers (matches engine expectation per scripts 015, 016, 067, 068). Second scenario uses a new script step starting in declare_attackers again at turn 5. Persist ETB chain: first, Kitchen Finks' persist trigger resolves (returns Kitchen Finks to battlefield). This causes Kitchen Finks' ETB trigger (gain 2 life) to trigger. That ETB trigger goes on the stack next. Both players pass → ETB resolves → P1 gains 2 life. Assert: P1 at 42 life, Kitchen Finks on battlefield as 2/1 with -1/-1 counter. HARNESS VALIDATION FAILURES (2026-02-26): (1) Kitchen Finks CardDefinition missing — Persist and ETB triggers never fire; engine treats it as a vanilla creature. (2) After the first combat_damage step ends, the engine does not automatically advance to a new turn — issuing DeclareAttackers again fails with InvalidCommand('DeclareAttackers is only valid in the DeclareAttackers step'). The script is CR-correct; both failures are engine/definition gaps recorded in the disputes array.

disputes.description: Kitchen Finks is not in the engine Available Cards list and has no CardDefinition. This script will fail harness validation until Kitchen Finks is authored with its Persist triggered ability and ETB life-gain effect. Without the CardDefinition, the engine treats Kitchen Finks as a vanilla creature — Persist never triggers, ETB gain-2-life never fires, and life-gain assertions will show 40 instead of 42. The script is otherwise CR-correct per oracle text (Scryfall verified via MCP).

Harness error: InvalidCommand('DeclareAttackers is only valid in the DeclareAttackers step'). The script models two separate combats across two turns within one script file. After the first combat_damage step completes, the engine does not automatically advance the game to a new turn's declare_attackers step. The harness has no mechanism (e.g., an 'advance_turn' action) to move between turns mid-script. The second combat block (steps 3-5: declare_attackers, declare_blockers, combat_damage for Elvish Mystic) is CR-correct but cannot execute until the harness supports multi-turn scripting or an explicit turn-advance action. This is a LOW-severity engine/harness gap.

script.step_note: Turn 4: P2 declares Bog Raiders as attacker targeting P1. Bog Raiders taps (CR 508.1f). No attack triggers. P1 controls no Swamps, so Bog Raiders' Swampwalk is irrelevant.

actions.description: Initial: Kitchen Finks (3/2) on P1 battlefield (untapped); Bog Raiders (2/2) and Elvish Mystic (1/1) on P2 battlefield (untapped); life totals 40/40; stack empty
actions.note: Kitchen Finks (3/2, Persist) is untapped and free of summoning sickness (placed in initial state via builder). Bog Raiders (2/2, Swampwalk) is a legal attacker. P1 controls no Swamps, so Swampwalk does not restrict Kitchen Finks from blocking Bog Raiders.

CR 508.1: P2 declares Bog Raiders as attacker targeting P1. CR 508.1f: Bog Raiders taps as a result of attacking. Bog Raiders has no attack triggers. P1 controls no Swamps, so Swampwalk is irrelevant — Kitchen Finks can legally block Bog Raiders.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Bog Raiders is tapped, stack empty, Kitchen Finks still untapped on P1 battlefield
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Bog Raiders tapped upon attacking. No attack triggers (Bog Raiders has no triggered abilities). Stack is empty.
CR 116.3: P2 (active player) passes priority first, then P1 passes. Both pass with empty stack → advance to declare_blockers step.

script.step_note: P1 declares Kitchen Finks as blocker for Bog Raiders. Bog Raiders becomes blocked. Both players pass → advance to combat damage.

actions.note: CR 509.1: P1 (defending player) declares Kitchen Finks as a blocker for Bog Raiders. CR 509.1a: Kitchen Finks is untapped — legal blocker. CR 509.1g: Kitchen Finks becomes a blocking creature blocking Bog Raiders. CR 509.1h: Bog Raiders has exactly one creature declared as blocker — it becomes a blocked creature. Blockers do not tap upon blocking declaration.

actions.description: After declare_blockers: Kitchen Finks is not tapped, Bog Raiders is blocked, stack empty
actions.note: Kitchen Finks remains untapped after blocking declaration. Bog Raiders is now a blocked creature. Stack is empty (no triggers from blocking).

CR 116.3: P2 (active player) passes priority first, then P1. Both pass with empty stack in declare_blockers step → advance to combat damage step. Engine executes combat damage as a turn-based action: Bog Raiders (power 2) deals 2 damage to Kitchen Finks (toughness 2 — lethal); Kitchen Finks (power 3) deals 3 damage to Bog Raiders (toughness 2 — lethal). Damage is simultaneous (CR 510.2). SBAs will destroy both (CR 704.5g).

script.step_note: Combat damage step: Bog Raiders (power 2) deals 2 damage to Kitchen Finks (toughness 2 — lethal). Kitchen Finks (power 3) deals 3 damage to Bog Raiders (toughness 2 — lethal). CR 510.2: simultaneous. SBA 704.5g destroys both. Persist trigger fires for Kitchen Finks (had no -1/-1 counters) — requires CardDefinition. P1 takes no combat damage (attacker was blocked).

results.description: Kitchen Finks has 2 damage marked (equals toughness 2) — lethal damage — destroyed
Bog Raiders has 3 damage marked (exceeds toughness 2) — lethal damage — destroyed

actions.note: CR 704.5g: Kitchen Finks (toughness 2) has 2 damage marked — lethal — destroyed. Bog Raiders (toughness 2) has 3 damage marked — lethal — destroyed. CR 704.3: SBAs are checked as a batch simultaneously. Both creatures die simultaneously. Kitchen Finks' Persist trigger fires (per CR 702.79a, once CardDefinition is authored): 'When this permanent is put into a graveyard from the battlefield, if it had no -1/-1 counters on it...' — Kitchen Finks had no -1/-1 counters when it died, so the intervening-if condition is satisfied at trigger time (CR 603.4). Trigger goes on the stack.

actions.description: After SBAs: Kitchen Finks and Bog Raiders both in graveyard; P1 life still 40 (blocked attacker deals no player damage); Persist trigger on stack pending CardDefinition
actions.note: CR 510.1c: Bog Raiders (blocked) assigned all damage to Kitchen Finks — P1 takes 0 player damage. Both creatures are destroyed and in graveyards. Stack count assertion omitted here: once CardDefinition is authored, Persist trigger will be on stack (count=1); without def, stack will be empty (count=0). Assert only zone membership and life totals which are engine-independent.

CR 116.3: P2 (active player) passes first, then P1. Once Kitchen Finks CardDefinition exists: Persist trigger resolves, returning Kitchen Finks to battlefield with -1/-1 counter. ETB trigger then fires (gain 2 life). Without CardDefinition: stack is empty, no triggers fire.

resolution.note: CR 702.79a: Persist resolves — Kitchen Finks returns to the battlefield under P1's control with a -1/-1 counter on it (effective P/T: 2/1). CR 400.7: Kitchen Finks is a new object. ETB ability triggers: 'When this creature enters, you gain 2 life' — ETB trigger placed on stack. Requires CardDefinition to execute.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Persist trigger resolves. Requires Kitchen Finks CardDefinition. Without it, this stack_resolve is a no-op annotation.

actions.description: After Persist resolves (requires CardDefinition): Kitchen Finks back on P1 battlefield as 2/1 with -1/-1 counter; ETB trigger on stack
actions.note: CR 702.79a: Kitchen Finks returned to battlefield with -1/-1 counter (effective P/T: 2/1). CR 400.7: new object. ETB trigger pending on stack. These assertions will only pass once the Kitchen Finks CardDefinition is authored with Persist and ETB gain-2-life.
CR 116.3: P2 (active player) passes first, then P1. ETB trigger (gain 2 life) resolves. Requires CardDefinition.

resolution.note: CR 118.2: P1 gains 2 life from Kitchen Finks' ETB trigger. P1 life: 40 + 2 = 42. Requires CardDefinition.

actions.note: CR 608.2: ETB triggered ability resolves. P1 gains 2 life (40 → 42). Stack now empty. Requires Kitchen Finks CardDefinition.
CR 116.3: P2 passes first, then P1. Both pass with empty stack. End of first combat scenario. Second combat scenario (steps 3-5) requires harness turn-advance support — see dispute.

actions.description: End of first combat: Kitchen Finks on P1 battlefield as 2/1 (with -1/-1 counter); Bog Raiders in P2 graveyard; Elvish Mystic still on P2 battlefield; P1 at 42 life (after ETB gain); stack empty
actions.note: CR 702.79a: Persist triggered — Kitchen Finks returned as 2/1 with -1/-1 counter (requires CardDefinition). CR 118.2: P1 gained 2 life from ETB trigger (40 → 42, requires CardDefinition). Life assertion omitted from this block since it depends on Kitchen Finks CardDefinition. Bog Raiders destroyed; Elvish Mystic intact for second combat.

script.step_note: Turn 5: P2 declares Elvish Mystic as attacker targeting P1. NOTE: This step requires harness support for turn advancement between combat scenarios. The engine will reject DeclareAttackers here because the game phase has not advanced past the first combat_damage step. See dispute for harness gap. CR-correct per rules: Kitchen Finks (2/1 with -1/-1 counter) is a legal blocker in the new turn.

actions.note: CR 508.1: P2 declares Elvish Mystic as attacker targeting P1. CR 508.1f: Elvish Mystic taps as a result of attacking. Kitchen Finks (2/1 with -1/-1 counter, persisted in previous turn) remains untapped and can block. Requires harness turn-advance support — will fail with InvalidCommand until then (see dispute).

actions.description: After declare_attackers (turn 5): Elvish Mystic is tapped; Kitchen Finks untapped on P1 battlefield; stack empty
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Elvish Mystic tapped upon attacking. No attack triggers. Stack is empty.
CR 116.3: P2 (active player) passes first, then P1. Both pass → advance to declare_blockers step.

script.step_note: P1 declares Kitchen Finks (2/1 with -1/-1 counter) as blocker for Elvish Mystic. Both players pass → advance to combat damage.

actions.note: CR 509.1: P1 declares Kitchen Finks (now 2/1 with -1/-1 counter) as a blocker for Elvish Mystic. CR 509.1a: Kitchen Finks is untapped — legal blocker. CR 509.1g: Kitchen Finks becomes a blocking creature. CR 509.1h: Elvish Mystic becomes a blocked creature.

CR 116.3: P2 (active player) passes first, then P1. Both pass → advance to combat damage step. Engine executes combat damage: Elvish Mystic (power 1) deals 1 damage to Kitchen Finks (effective toughness 1 after -1/-1 counter — lethal); Kitchen Finks (effective power 2 after -1/-1 counter) deals 2 damage to Elvish Mystic (toughness 1 — lethal). Simultaneous (CR 510.2).

script.step_note: Second combat damage: Elvish Mystic (1/1) deals 1 damage to Kitchen Finks (effective toughness 1). Kitchen Finks (effective power 2) deals 2 damage to Elvish Mystic (toughness 1). Both take lethal damage — both die. Persist trigger would fire but intervening-if fails: Kitchen Finks HAD a -1/-1 counter (CR 702.79a + CR 603.4). Kitchen Finks stays in graveyard permanently.

results.description: Kitchen Finks has 1 damage marked (equals effective toughness 1 after -1/-1 counter) — lethal damage — destroyed
Elvish Mystic has 2 damage marked (exceeds toughness 1) — lethal damage — destroyed

actions.note: CR 704.5g: Kitchen Finks (effective toughness 1 from base 2 minus -1/-1 counter) has 1 damage marked — lethal — destroyed. Elvish Mystic (toughness 1) has 2 damage marked — lethal — destroyed. CR 704.3: Both SBAs applied simultaneously. Kitchen Finks dies for the second time. Persist trigger would check at trigger time (CR 702.79a intervening-if): 'if it had no -1/-1 counters on it' — Kitchen Finks HAD a -1/-1 counter from its first persist return. Condition FAILS. Persist does NOT trigger (CR 603.4: intervening-if prevents the ability from going on the stack). Kitchen Finks remains in the graveyard permanently.
CR 116.3: P2 (active player) passes first, then P1. Both pass after combat damage and SBAs. No triggers on the stack (persist did not fire — intervening-if failed). Stack is empty.

actions.description: Final state: Kitchen Finks permanently in P1 graveyard (persist did not trigger — had -1/-1 counter); Elvish Mystic in P2 graveyard; P1 at 42 life; P2 at 40 life; both battlefields empty of creatures; stack empty
actions.note: CR 702.79a: Persist's intervening-if clause ('if it had no -1/-1 counters on it') checked at trigger time. Kitchen Finks had a -1/-1 counter from its previous persist return — condition fails — persist does NOT trigger. CR 603.4: Intervening-if is evaluated both when the trigger would trigger AND when it resolves. Since it fails at trigger time, the ability never goes on the stack. Kitchen Finks remains in the graveyard permanently. Life assertion (P1=42) omitted here since it depends on Kitchen Finks CardDefinition resolving the ETB gain-2-life in the first combat. P2 life is 40 (no unblocked combat damage reached P1 or P2 during either combat scenario).
